title: RNAScope in situ hybridization (ISH) multiplex fluorescent protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8tlhc","text":"Instructions to perform ISH using RNAscope kit from acdbio company ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":52,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"1BF8738B129311EDB0F00A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://acdbio.com"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2b7jt","text":"ISH is used to detect RNA. Thus, it is very important that all the reagents, equipment, and bench space used are very clean and uncontaminated.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":143}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"674v6","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dq9up","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8k8r0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5jfh","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"51gou","text":"For Fixed Frozen Brain samples ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"65pba","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1g83c","text":"The challenge with thicker sections (30 to 40 microns) is tissue attachment. Hence, there are additional fixation and baking steps with respect to the standard protocol.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"82t38","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1nqpp","text":"- Cut 30 μm (or 40 μm) sections. ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3gbhe","text":"- Collect sections into 12-24 well plates in anti-freeze solution","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5fivs","text":"- Store sections at -20oC for no more than 3-4 weeks; at 4°C for no more than 2 days.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":23,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"df13s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3htiv","text":"For Fixed Frozen Nodose Ganglion samples ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":41}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b90ra","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9fo9i","text":"- Cut 14 to 20 μm sections in cryostat. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eqc6s","text":"- Collect sections into SuperFrost Plus Gold slides, avoid more than 3 sections per slide, and OCT overlapping with tissue from a previous section.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"96gce","text":"- Let sections dry at room temperature for 1 hour.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3q22d","text":"- Store sections at -80oC for no more than 4-5 weeks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":23,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dsstk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"agl95","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Select tissue – ~ 3 sections/sample.
Dry tissue at Room temperature for 01:00:00 to 02:00:00 . Cover the slide loosely with foil to avoid contaminations.
Select tissue – ~ 3 sections/sample.
Wash in PBS 1X to remove agar or OCT for 2X 00:15:00 . Wash free-floating sections into net-wells and black tray.
Dry at Room temperature for 01:00:00 - cover the slides loosely with foil to avoid contaminations.
Rinse in ddH2O by moving slide rack up and down (3-5 times) for 3x 00:00:15 .
Dry at Room temperature for 01:00:00 - cover the slides loosely with foil to avoid contaminations.
Bake at 60 °C for 01:00:00 .
During this incubation, prepare 4% PFA and keep in the fridge 4 °C .
Post-fix in 4% PFA at 4 °C for 01:00:00 (in the fridge).
During this time prepare the following buffers:

Make 1X target retrieval buffer RNAscope Target Retrieval (x10) ACD Catalog #322001 . (Warm stock solution 10X in water bath at 40°C for ~10 min before diluting / 1 mL 10X stock solution + 9 mL ddH 2 0 )

Make 1X wash buffer ( provided in RNAscope Fluorescent Multiplex Reagent Kit ACD Catalog #320850 ). - Warm 50X stock solution in water bath at 40°C for 10-20 min before diluting - 1mL 50X stock solution + 49 mL ddH 2 0. MIX WELL!!! - 3L of 1X wash buffer -> 60 mL 50x stock solution (1 bottle) + 2.94 L ddH 2 0 - Store 1X wash buffer at RT for up to 1 month.
Prepare bottles with EtOH gradient solutions: 50%, 70%, and 100%.
TURN ON STEAMER and SETUP at 100 °C . Use thermometer to check temperature.
Dry at 60 °C for 00:15:00 .
Add RNAscope hydrogen peroxide - incubate slides at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
If active bubbling happens, please extend the Room temperature incubation to 00:20:00 .
15. Rinse in ddH2O by moving slide rack up and down (3-5 times) for 2 x 00:00:15 .
Dry at 60 °C for 00:15:00 .
PLACE JARS WITH ddH2O AND TARGET RETRIEVAL (TR) SOLUTION INTO STEAMER 00:15:00 before is needed. Before starting the target retrieval step make sure that the TR buffer is not actively bubbling.
TR step in the steamer -> place slides in ddH2O jar for 15 sec then move them into TR solution jar for 00:10:00 100 °C .
Rinse in ddH2O 00:00:15 by moving slide rack up and down (3-5 times).
Rinse in ddH2O by moving slide rack up and down (3-5 times) for 2 x 00:00:15 .
After this step, proceed IMMEDIATELLY to the RNAscope assay.

*NOTE – After each step (most important after washes)- Remove excess liquid from the slides before adding new reagents by tapping or gently flicking the slide on absorbent paper. If you have a hydrophobic barrier, you can also use a Kimwipe to absorb the left-over liquid. Tilting the slide slightly and allowing solutions to pool in one corner on the hydrophobic barrier to then dab with a Kimwipe works well. Dehydration steps using EtOH gradient: 50%, 70%, 100%, new 100% - at Room temperature , 5 min/each.

PROBE HYBRIDIZATION Add (4-6) drops of probe mixture to appropriate slide(s), add positive probe to positive control slide, add negative probe to negative control slide. Incubate at 40 °C for 02:00:00 .
During this time prepare 5x SSC buffer: 1.25mL 20x SSC + 3.75mL ddH20 5x SSC buffer can be stored for up to 2 months at RT
Rinse with 1X wash buffer for 2x 00:02:00 .
Cover tissue with 5x SSC, incubate at Room temperature Overnight in covered humidified chamber.
DAY 2 TURN ON HYBRIDIZATION OVEN and setup it at 40 °C .
Remove 5x SSC solution.
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .
Add drops of Amp #1 at 40 °C for 00:30:00 (in humidified chamber into hybridization oven).
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of Amp #2 at 40 °C for 3 00:30:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of Amp #3 at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
During this time, dilute Opal fluorophores (range 1:750-1:3K ; usually dil. 1:1500) in TSA buffer :
Determine volume needed (150-220 ul/slide). Example: 0.5μL fluorophore in 750μL TSA buffer from kit
Use tinfoil to cover tubes with the solutions as fluorophores are light sensitive. NOTE: if Opal 690 is assigned to C1 or C2, its concentration may need to be increased.
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of HRP-C1 at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of diluted Opal selected for C1 at 40 °C for 00:30:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of HRP-blocker at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of HRP-C2 at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of diluted Opal selected for C2 at 40 °C for 00:30:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of HRP-blocker at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of HRP-C3 at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of diluted Opal selected for C3 at 40 °C for 00:30:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of HRP-blocker at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of HRP-C4 at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of diluted Opal selected for C4 at at 40 °C for 00:30:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
Add drops of HRP-blocker at 40 °C for 00:15:00 .
Rinse with 1X wash buffer at Room temperature for 2x 00:02:00 .
DAPI solution from RNAscope kit at Room temperature for 00:00:30 .
Air dry slides briefly, covered (slides are light sensitive).
Coverslip with ProLong Gold Antifade Mountant (from Thermo Fisher) or other comparable fluorescent mountant.
Immediately store slides in a light-proof box at 4 °C Dry 30 min to O/N.
Do not leave out at RT.
To prolong the signal, clear nail polish may be used to seal the coverslip to the slide.
Image slides after 8 hours or within 2 weeks. Signal deteriorates fast.
Dip in 100% EtOH at Room temperature for 00:03:00 .

Draw hydrophobic barrier around the tissue: Using the hydrophobic pen, ImmEdge hydrophobic barrier pap pen Vector Laboratories Catalog #H-4000 (RRID: AB_2336517) , make a barrier as close to tissue as possible (without touching it) to reduce the amount of solution needed to cover the surface area. While waiting for pen to dry, PRE-HEAT OVEN and SLIDE TRAY AT 40 °C .

Add (4-6) drops of protease III (provided in RNAscope Fluorescent Multiplex Reagent Kit ACD Catalog #320850 RNAscope Protease III ACD Catalog #322337 ) , digest in humidity chamber at 40 °C for 00:30:00 .
During protease incubation:

Equilibrate amplification reagents (provided in RNAscope Fluorescent Multiplex Reagent Kit ACD Catalog #320850 ) : Remove AMP1, AMP2, AMP3, HPR-C1, HPR-C2, HPR-C3, HPR blockers from the fridge and equilibrate at RT.

Prepare the probes (provided in RNAscope Fluorescent Multiplex Reagent Kit ACD Catalog #320850 ) Warm probes and probe stock solution in water bath at 40 °C for 00:10:00 cool at Room temperature Briefly spin probe stock solutions Mix probes into a new tube - Probe ratios = C2:C3:C4:C1 = 1:1:1:50. Example: 4μL C3 + 4μL C2 + 192 μL C1 If not using a C1 probe, use RNAScope probe diluent in its place Invert tube several times to mix before use. Store mixed probes at 2 °C - 8 °C for up to 6 months.
Wash in PBS 1X to remove OCT for 2X 00:15:00 .
Mount tissue on superfrost charged gold slides.
During this incubation, PRE-HEAT HYBRIDIZATION OVEN and SLIDE TRAY at 60 °C .
creator: Jacquelyn Haytayan
